pattern: A single frequency count identifies which strings are distinct, and then a second left-to-right pass in original order picks out the k-th one among just those.
hints: Count how many times each string appears in the array -- anything with a count of exactly one is 'distinct'.
Walk the array again in its original order and collect only the distinct strings into a new list.
If that list has at least k entries, return the k-th one (1-indexed); otherwise there aren't enough distinct strings, so return the empty string.

edge_inputs.description: all strings identical, nothing is distinct
k equals the array length, only one distinct element at the very end
distinct strings appear out of the order they'd sort in
k equals the exact count of distinct strings available
k is one more than the number of distinct strings, forcing the empty-string result

stress.description: random short lowercase strings with a random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    arr = [''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 5))) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [arr, k]
